mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month growth in real retail sales (RSAFS) signals robust consumer spending, which drives corporate earnings higher and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the following month.
failure_condition: Rank IC of RSAFS month-over-month change against 20-day forward returns is below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive changes are followed by negative returns more than 50% of the time.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) above 0.2 percentage points signals weakening labor demand, which depresses consumer income and spending, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: After a UNRATE trigger, 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of instances, or the mean return is not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the effective federal funds rate falls below the trailing 12-month CPI inflation rate, real interest rates become negative, which reduces the discount rate on equity cash flows and encourages risk-taking, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction signal (negative real rate) produces positive 20-day returns less than 55% of the time in walk-forward tests, or the mean return is not significantly greater than zero (one-sided t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: During late-cycle expansion when capacity is tight, further increases in industrial production (INDPRO) signal overheating and input cost pressures, which compress profit margins and lead to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion (output gap positive, capacity utilization above 80%)
failure_condition: In periods identified as late-cycle expansion, a rising INDPRO is followed by positive 20-day returns more than 50% of the time, or the negative mean return is not significantly different from zero.

mechanism_narrative: Strong monthly payroll gains (PAYEMS) indicate a healthy labor market with rising aggregate income, which boosts consumer spending and corporate earnings, yielding positive short-term S&P 500 forward returns within the next week.
failure_condition: The correlation between PAYEMS month-over-month change and 5-day forward returns is negative or a rank IC below 0.02 over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp monthly decline in housing starts (HOUST) exceeding 5% signals deteriorating residential investment and weakening consumer confidence, which foreshadows broader economic softening and negative S&P 500 returns over the following month.
regime_hint: housing-sensitive periods (e.g., post-2008 recovery or rate hike cycles)
failure_condition: After a HOUST decline trigger, 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 60% of instances, or the average return is not significantly negative (one-sided test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month declines in industrial production (INDPRO) and nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) indicate a synchronized slowdown in both manufacturing and labor markets, which historically precedes or confirms recessions and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO month-over-month change < 0 AND PAYEMS month-over-month change < 0
regime_hint: early recession or significant economic contraction
failure_condition: When both proxies decline, 60-day forward returns are positive more than 40% of the time, or the mean return is not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: In a monetary tightening cycle characterized by rising FEDFUNDS and above-target inflation, any further acceleration in CPI (CPIAUCSL) reinforces hawkish expectations, increasing long-term discount rates and compressing equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next week.
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle (FEDFUNDS increasing and CPIAUCSL YoY > 2%)
failure_condition: Within the identified tightening regimes, a rising CPIAUCSL is not followed by negative 5-day returns more than 55% of the time, or the average return fails to be significantly negative.
